# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Envoy proxy configuration for HTTP traffic routing. Define listeners on port 80, clusters for backend services, and routes to match requests. Configure connection pooling and health checks for upstream services. This is for our API gateway proxy.
# Category: envoy_no_tls

listeners:
- name: listener_http   # Name of the listner  - "listener" will be a unique identifier by Envoy Proxy, must not exceed length limit (128 bytes) and cannot include ':', '..' or '/'. The characters are limited to [a-zA-Z0-9_-].
  address: any   # This makes the listener listen on all addresses. Change if you need a specific IP for Envoy Proxy, must be either an IPv4/IPv6 format in string form after 'tcp:' protocol is included and it should not exceed length limit (128 bytes) - example "0:0:0:0" or 192.50.37.2
  port_specifier: 80   # The listener listens on the specified ports, must be a value between [DEFAULT = 64] and range of (MAX = DEFAULT + ~[envoy-default=1]) to use ENVOY default for all listeners where it can't determine otherwise
clusters:    # Defines Cluster which Envoy will route requests. The number or type must be unique, length limit [256], characters are limited in ['A'- 'Z', '_','a'-...,'z']. Example "cluster_name"  - No two clusters should have the same name
- name: cluster1   # Cluster to use when routing requests. This is a reference by Envoy Proxy and must match exactly one or many names in an upstream list which matches 'clusters'. Must not exceed length limit (256). Example "cluster_name" - No two clusters should have the same name
  connect_timeout: 0   # Timeout to use when establishing a connection if none established within this period. Default is no timeout, must be between [1ms-3hrs] and cannot exceed length limit (5s) or set it as 'none'. Value can not include '-' in value
backend_type: round_robin   # This specifies the type of balancing that Envoy Proxy will use to distribute incoming requests. Can be one from [LEAST_CONNECTIONS, ROUND_ROBIN] or ['leastConnections', 'roundRobin']. Default is LEAST_CONNECTIONS
health_checks:   # This section contains the configurations for how Envoy will health check backends. There can be multiple items in this block and each item has to match exactly one name of a Cluster defined before with an upstream list, which matches 'clusters'. Must not exceed length limit (256). Example "health_check" - No two clusters should have the same health check
  interval: 10s   # Interval at witch Envoy will send HealthCheck requests to backend. Default is no timeout or set it as 'none' and cannot be less than [3ms]. Value can not include '-', '/','..': Example "interval", value="5mins". Must exceed length limit (10s).
  timeout: auto   # Timeout before giving up on health check if none established within this period. Default is no timeout, must be either an ISO-8601 Duration format or 'none', Cannot set it less than [3ms] and cannot greater that ['5mins' for instance]. Value can not include '-','..': Example "timeout", value="2s".
  success: 200   # This is the expected response code Envoy should use when considered successful. Default in 'auto'. It must be a number between [1-9] followed by an optional digit or key from {'successful', ..., unhealthy}. Example "http_status" - No two clusters and health checks have same http status to consider as healthy example: { success = 200 }.
  timeout: auto   # Timeout before considering a backend 'unhealthy' if none established within this period (used for HTTP/HTTPS probes). Default is no duration, must be either an ISO-8601 Duration format or "none". Cannot set it less than [3ms]. Value can not include '-', '/','..': Example: 
    interval:"2s", timeout="5mins";; Auto as default. Both fields are mutually exclusive and cannot be both 'auto'. It must exceed length limit (10 seconds). This field also does auto fill in for http_status which is not specified manually here, to set it can only use numbers between [3ms], or ['none', { success = 200 }]. Example: timeout="5mins".
